markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Comprehensive testing of Firecrawl MCP connector revealing 8 available tools for web scraping and data extraction. Successfully demonstrated scrape, search, map, and extract functions with real-world examples. All core features tested successfully including single page content extraction, web search, URL mapping, and schema-based structured data extraction.


## Context & Intent

User requested help testing the Firecrawl connector to understand its capabilities and see practical demonstrations of its features.


## What Was Done

Systematically tested 4 core Firecrawl tools: scraped Wikipedia article, searched for AI trends articles, mapped Firecrawl documentation URLs, and extracted structured iPhone product data using custom schema. Documented all 8 available tools and their functions.


## Outputs Produced

- [test_report] Firecrawl connector capabilities overview — Complete documentation of 8 available tools with practical test results
- [scraped_content] Wikipedia web scraping article — Clean markdown content extracted from single page
- [search_results] AI trends 2026 articles — 3 relevant URLs from Microsoft, IBM, and Forbes
- [url_mapping] Firecrawl documentation structure — 10 discovered pages including multilingual content
- [structured_data] iPhone 16 product information — Schema-based extraction of name, features, and pricing

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Tested 4 most critical tools first
- Used diverse real-world examples to demonstrate versatility
- Created custom schema for structured extraction test

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- All 4 tested tools performed successfully
- Firecrawl provides clean, structured output
- LLM-based extraction enables flexible data parsing
- Good performance and caching capabilities
Discoveries:

- 8 tools available including autonomous agent features
- Supports multiple output formats
- Includes advanced search operators
- Can handle both simple and complex scraping scenarios

## Open Questions

- How do the untested crawl and agent tools perform?
- What are the rate limits and usage constraints?
- How does performance scale with large crawling jobs?

## Next Steps

- Test remaining 4 tools (crawl, agent, status checkers)
- Explore advanced configuration options
- Integrate into larger workflows
- Test performance with larger datasets
